adds.rationale: Robotics is the first-named next tech wave in the profile and has zero watchlist coverage; BOTZ gained 16% in November 2023 and 29% YTD — an early-surge signal — while NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL give the optimizer enough AI exposure to absorb AAPL's removal.

news_evidence.summary: Global X Robotics & AI ETF (BOTZ) surged 16% in November 2023 (vs S&P 500 +9%), up 29% YTD, on Nvidia's AI tailwind and rate-cut expectations.

removes.rationale: Apple posted its fourth consecutive quarter of declining revenue — its longest slump in 22 years — with Mac sales down ~34% YoY and flat holiday guidance; MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA already cover the AI wave, making AAPL the weakest-thesis slot and the natural pair for a robotics add.

news_evidence.summary: Apple Q4 FY2023 revenue fell for the fourth straight quarter (longest slump in 22 years), Mac -34% YoY, Greater China flat amid Huawei competition; no growth expected in the holiday quarter.
Apple Q4 2023: revenue $89.5B (-1% YoY), every hardware line outside iPhone declined, Mac -34%; shares fell 3%+ after-hours on weak holiday guidance.

rationale_overall: The watchlist entered Q4 2023 full at 8/8 with four AI names and zero robotics coverage — an obvious thematic gap. Swapping AAPL (four straight quarterly revenue declines, Mac in freefall, no AI thesis distinct from MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA) for BOTZ opens the robotics bucket while keeping AI represented by three anchors. Remaining holdings confirmed: RKLB won a $515M US Space Force satellite contract (SEC filing Dec 21, 2023) and flew a successful return-to-flight Electron Dec 14; URA reinforced by COP28's Dec 2, 2023 pledge by 22 nations to triple nuclear capacity by 2050 (uranium spot up ~88% YTD); QTUM validated by quantum momentum (IONQ ~+290% in 2023). Defense and demographics remain unrepresented, to be revisited when a remove creates room.
